basics.name: Narain Karthik
basics.label: Full-stack developer
basics.summary: Full-stack developer and Cloud/DevOps practitioner focused on scalable, high-performance applications.

work.name: Vegas Consulting
work.summary: Experienced in developing high-complexity, scalable, data-driven front-end projects with a focus on performance, user experience, and system integration.
responsibilities: Developed scalable front-end applications using modern JavaScript frameworks, improving page load performance by up to 35%
Designed reusable and responsive UI components, reducing duplicate code and improving development efficiency by 30%
Optimized front-end performance, accessibility, and SEO, increasing Lighthouse scores by 25%+
Integrated business logic with data-driven user interfaces, reducing UI-related defects by 20%
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to deliver production-ready features within defined timelines
achievements: Implemented CI/CD pipelines to automate build and deployment processes, reducing manual deployment effort by 60%
Managed cloud application deployments on Google Cloud Platform and Microsoft Azure, improving environment stability by 40%
Applied Git repository governance using branch protections and rulesets, reducing production issues caused by improper merges by 50%
Improved deployment reliability and release consistency through DevOps automation, decreasing rollback incidents by 30%

projects.name: Fit Track
projects.description: Modern MERN fitness log with responsive dashboard, built for athletes who need speed and insight without friction.

caseStudy.usage: Users log workouts quickly, view streaks and analytics, and manage custom routines across devices.
tradeoffs: Full-stack MERN architecture increases onboarding complexity but ensures end-to-end control.
Redux Toolkit and optimistic updates boost responsiveness at the cost of bundle size.
Material‑UI theming accelerates design consistency but couples UI to one component library.
technicalNeeds: Indexed Mongo collections and memoized selectors for fast filtering.
Express API with JWT auth, Mongoose models and standard REST endpoints.
Docker‑Compose configuration for development parity across frontend and backend.
DX: Monorepo‑style repo layout with shared env conventions and scripts.
Preconfigured ESLint/Prettier, Conventional Commit guidelines, CI/CD via GitHub Actions.
UX: Minimal data entry friction with schema-driven forms and heatmap cues.
Mobile‑first layout, instant charts, and progress summaries for habit formation.

projects.name: Sticky Memo
projects.description: Write freely, knowing your thoughts are yours alone—privacy‑first, local‑only note taking with rich views and zero server dependency.

caseStudy.usage: Capture ideas fast, switch between grid/kanban/table/roadmap, and export when needed—all from the browser.
tradeoffs: Offline‑first local storage precludes built‑in sync or collaboration.
Lightweight formatting and no analytics prioritize speed over features.
technicalNeeds: Durable persistence using IndexedDB/localStorage with debounced writes.
Pluggable views and drag‑and‑drop controllers for flexible organisation.
DX: Clear folder structure (components, controllers, services) and TypeScript models.
No-env setup reduces friction; simple build scripts and conventions.
UX: Instant response, low cognitive load, trust built by silence (no tracking).
Multiple view modes support individual workflows.

projects.name: Nmoji
projects.description: Instant emoji search and picker built on Astro—leveraging static generation for lightning‑fast client side filtering and a companion browser extension.

caseStudy.usage: Type or navigate with keyboard to filter thousands of emojis, then copy with a single keystroke.
tradeoffs: Bundling the full emoji dataset inflates initial payload but guarantees zero‑latency search.
Tight metadata taxonomy keeps UI simple at the expense of niche classification.
technicalNeeds: Build‑time index generation and lightweight fuzzy matching on the client.
Astro for static pages, React components for interactive UI, and extension packaging.
DX: Astro+React hybrid enables small runtime and familiar React workflows.
Clear directory layout, utility libs, and reusable UI components.
UX: Keyboard‑first flow, immediate feedback, and minimal distractions make lookup feel effortless.

projects.name: Contribution Cards
projects.description: Dynamic directory of GitHub contributors – filters, caches and surfaces active profiles across repositories without a server.

caseStudy.usage: Aggregate contributor data, apply filters, and render profile cards while respecting API rate limits.
tradeoffs: Aggressive caching adds complexity but is necessary to stay under GitHub rate limits.
Simplified scoring sacrifices deep analytics in favor of clarity and performance.
technicalNeeds: Dual‑layer in‑memory/localStorage cache with backoff/retry logic.
SWR pattern for stale‑while‑revalidate data fetching.
DX: Modular hooks, controllers and clear docs (CACHING_AND_RATE_LIMITING.md) support contributor growth.
UX: Instant filtering, consistent card layout, and low‑noise data presentation.

projects.name: Readme Shop
projects.description: Interactive README builder with live markdown preview, template library and badge/metric components—accelerating documentation creation.

caseStudy.usage: Compose sections from templates, preview Markdown in real time, then copy or embed the result.
tradeoffs: Opinionated templates speed authoring but constrain bespoke layouts.
Live preview requires continuous parsing but builds confidence for authors.
technicalNeeds: Markdown pipeline using Marked and DOMPurify, with clipboard hooks.
Zustand store for global state and modular template components.
DX: Design system alignment (Wisdom Fox), semantic release, and rich CLI scripts.
UX: Guided assembly, visible focus states, and responsive UI for both desktop and mobile.
